author: WebSurfer
text:
I couldn’t complete the archival comparison because I was not able to retrieve the actual Wayback snapshots for **March 22, 2021** and **April 21, 2021** from the sources available through web search here.

What I *did* verify from a live Virtue menu page is the current “dinner menu” page and its **Large Rations** list:

- URL: `https://www.virtuerestaurant.com/menu/dine-in-small-large-rations-copy/`
- Exact text:
  - “## Large Rations”
  - “* cabbage … **$26**”
  - “* fish & grits … **$35**”
  - “* golden tilefish … **$35**”
  - “* salmon* … **$43**”
  - “* half chicken … **$29**”
  - “* short rib … **$42**”
  - “* ny strip steak* … **$69**”
  - “* blackened catfish … **$30**”

However, since I did **not** obtain the archived March 22 and April 21 versions from the Wayback Machine, I can’t honestly identify which main course was present on March 22 but absent on April 21.

If you want, I can keep going and try a more targeted archive lookup strategy, but I’d need to directly access the Wayback capture URLs or a working CDX listing first.

author: WebSurfer
text:
I couldn’t reliably complete this from the available web tools because the Wayback snapshot pages for the specific dates did not expose the archived menu text in a readable way, and the search results did not return the actual March 22, 2021 and April 21, 2021 captures.

What I *could* verify from the live Virtue dinner menu page is the current “Large Rations” list at:

- `https://www.virtuerestaurant.com/menu/dine-in-small-large-rations-copy/`

Exact text from that page:
- “## Large Rations”
- “* cabbage … **$26**”
- “* fish & grits … **$35**”
- “* golden tilefish … **$35**”
- “* salmon* … **$43**”
- “* half chicken … **$29**”
- “* short rib … **$42**”
- “* ny strip steak* … **$69**”
- “* blackened catfish … **$30**”

But because I do **not** have the concrete March 22, 2021 and April 21, 2021 archived menu text, I can’t honestly identify the item that appears on March 22 but not on April 21 without guessing.

If you want, I can keep trying with a more targeted archive-capture strategy, but I’d need a working Wayback CDX listing or the exact capture URLs to read.

author: ComputerTerminal
text:
I queried the Wayback Machine CDX API for Virtue’s menu pages around the requested dates and found usable snapshots.

## 1) CDX API results

### URL: `https://www.virtuerestaurant.com/menu/dine-in-small-large-rations-copy/`
Date range queried: `2021-03-01` to `2021-04-30`

Result:
- No captures returned in that range.

### URL: `https://www.virtuerestaurant.com/menus/`
Date range queried: `2021-03-01` to `2021-04-30`

Returned snapshots:
- `20210303170535` — `https://www.virtuerestaurant.com/menus/` — `200`
- `20210322130051` — `http://virtuerestaurant.com/menus/` — `301`
- `20210322130051` — `https://www.virtuerestaurant.com/menus/` — `200`
- `20210421080001` — `https://www.virtuerestaurant.com/menus/` — `200`

So the two relevant captures around your dates are:
- **2021-03-22 13:00:51**
- **2021-04-21 08:00:01**

## 2) Archived HTML fetches

I fetched the archived HTML for both Wayback captures and extracted the `Large Rations` section from the embedded JSON-LD menu data.

### Capture: `20210322130051`
Large Rations:
- cauliflower — cashew dukkah spice, root cellar vegetables, rice — **$21**
- Shrimp — marsh hen mill buttered grits — **$22**
- Salmon — white beans, turnip relish "chow chow", brown sugar glaze — **$25**
- Pot roast — creamed asparagus, roasted potatoes — **$32**
- Catfish — blackened, carolina gold rice, barbeque carrots — **$26**
- lemon chicken — roasted fennel, green beans, chicken jus — **$24**
- bbq pork steak — smothered cabbage, granny smith apple — **$30**

### Capture: `20210421080001`
